stories.headline: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

stories.headline: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
stories.summary: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

stories.headline: Trump Tells Congress Why He Doesn’t Need Its Authorization for the Iran War
stories.summary: In letters to the House and Senate, the president asserted that the hostilities had “terminated,” in an apparent attempt to avoid having to seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: A deadline for the Iran war is here. What does the War Powers Act say?
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asserted an ongoing ceasefire “pauses” the clock on the 60-day deadline to end hostilities in Iran or seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

stories.headline: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
stories.summary: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%
stories.summary: The current level of tariffs charged on goods entering the US from the EU is 15%, under a deal negotiated last July.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: Trump says he will raise tariff on EU vehicles to 25%
stories.summary: Levies on cars and trucks exported to US will be imposed ‘next week’ unless companies manufacture at American plants

stories.headline: Trump Threatens Higher Tariffs on European Cars
stories.summary: President Trump said that the European Union was not upholding its part of a trade agreement and that he would increase tariffs next week.

stories.headline: US withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany after Merz says US ‘humiliated’ by Iran
stories.summary: US president says European countries are ‘absolutely horrible’ to refuse to support operations in strait of Hormuz• Why does the US have military bases in Germany?• Trump tells Merz to ‘fix his broken country’ in new attack on German chancellorThe US is withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, the Pentagon announced on Friday, as Donald Trump also threatened Italy and Spain for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz.The president’s move to redu

stories.headline: Trump to downsize U.S. military presence in Germany
stories.summary: The decision coincides with a feud between the president and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, who has criticized the U.S. war in Iran.

stories.headline: Trump tears up part of EU tariff deal to raise import duties on cars and lorries
stories.summary: US president says tariff on vehicles imported from EU will rise to 25% and accuses bloc of non-complianceDonald Trump has said he is tearing up part of the tariff deal he struck with EU leaders at his golf course in Scotland last summer, criticising Brussels for taking so long to ratify the deal.Blindsiding Brussels late on Friday, a public holiday in much of Europe, he announced that he would be increasing tariffs on cars and lorries imported in

stories.headline: Trump says Iran seeks terms he ‘can’t agree to’ in latest peace proposal
stories.summary: Trump says the Iranian offer sent via Pakistan contains terms he's not satisfied with.

stories.headline: Trump announces 25 percent tariffs on European Union cars, trucks
stories.summary: US president says EU ‘not complying’ with current trade deal, launches new tariffs to begin next week.

stories.headline: Trump to raise US tariffs on EU cars to 25%, accusing bloc of not complying with deal
stories.summary: Trump and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen agreed to the trade deal last July which set a 15% tariff on most goods.

stories.headline: US President Donald Trump says 'not satisfied' with new peace proposal from Iran
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Washington is gripped by a legalistic debate over whether Trump has passed a deadline for requesting congressional approval for the war.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Seventeen people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.headline: US warns Europe of delays to arms shipments as Iran war drains stockpiles
stories.summary: Deferred deliveries could include those used in Ukraine’s defence against Russia

stories.headline: What to Know About the Maine Senate Race After Janet Mills Drops Out
stories.summary: The exit of Gov. Janet Mills kicked off the general election early in what is likely to be one of the most important, expensive and combative Senate races of 2026.

stories.headline: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
stories.summary: Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill affordThe Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said.Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and sho

stories.headline: Trump threatens to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain – as it happened
stories.summary: The US president said he would carry out of a review of US military presence in Europe after public criticism of the US-Israeli war on IranIf you are still planning your summer holidays and looking at some of perhaps more original ways of spending your time crossing Europe, you now have a new option in a train between Prague and Copenhagen via Berlin and Hamburg.The Czech operator, České dráhy, has been somewhat excitedly posting about the latest

stories.headline: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel's strikes continue, with more than 2,600 killed since March 2, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

stories.headline: US warns shippers against paying Strait of Hormuz tolls, ‘donations’
stories.summary: Latest warning comes as Iranian state media reports Tehran has presented new peace proposal to US.

stories.headline: Iran war-driven surge in oil prices pushes Japan toward yen intervention
Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
stories.summary: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Oil market one month from crunch point as global stockpiles dwindle
stories.summary: Traders warn of further jump in prices and ‘huge pain’ as economies cut consumption

stories.headline: Stocks Hit Record as Oil Falls on US-Iran Hopes
stories.summary: A record-breaking run drove stocks toward their longest weekly rally since 2024 amid hopes for a deal to end the Iran war that has rattled financial markets and jeopardized the economic outlook. Jaya Thibard Wajja, head of FX strategy at TD Securities, and Bloomberg's Jeff Mason join Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fact-Checking Claims on Political Violence After Latest Trump Assassination Attempt
stories.summary: Republican commentators have argued, incorrectly, that political violence is largely a left-wing problem. Liberals have countered by citing the threats made against former President Obama.

stories.headline: May Day Protests Pushing for Worker Protections Expected Across the U.S.
stories.summary: Organizers have billed the events as a nationwide day of action to demand policies that put the interests of working people over those of the ultrawealthy.

stories.headline: 18-Year-Old Accused of Killing 2 in Kentucky Bank Robbery
stories.summary: Two employees at a bank in Berea, Ky., were shot and killed. Law enforcement officers arrested the man after a chase, finding a gun in a wrecked BMW.

stories.headline: Billionaire Chris Larsen Plans to Spend $3.5 Million in NY House Race Amid Midterm Clash Over A.I.
stories.summary: Chris Larsen, who hails from California, plans to spend $3.5 million to help Alex Bores, a New York congressional candidate at the center of a proxy war over A.I. regulation.

stories.headline: EU trade deal with South America’s Mercosur bloc takes provisional effect
stories.summary: The long-awaited deal creates one of the world’s largest free trade areas with 720m potential consumers.

stories.headline: The KOSPI's miracle surge comes with a warning label
China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: MBZ, the Emirati sheikh who shook up the Gulf
stories.summary: From foreign interventions to abandoning Opec, the UAE’s leader is charting a different path for his country

stories.headline: Spirit Airlines prepares to cease operations as bailout talks founder
stories.summary: Trump says US still ‘looking’ at a rescue for bankrupt budget carrier but would only proceed ‘if it’s a good deal’

stories.headline: Columbia Risks Losing Top Credit Rating as Trump Slams Colleges
stories.summary: Columbia University’s credit outlook was revised to negative by Moody’s Ratings, which cited rising risks tied to the “federal environment for higher education.”

stories.headline: Emerging Assets Climb on Iran’s New Proposal in Thin Trading
stories.summary: Emerging-market benchmarks for stocks and currencies advanced in thin holiday trading, as Iran delivered a new proposal to the US via Pakistan over the Strait of Hormuz, pushing crude lower.

stories.headline: Brooks Apparel 'Resonating' With Chinese Runners, Says CEO Dan Sheridan
stories.summary: Dan Sheridan, CEO of Brooks Running, said that his company is resonating with runners in China as the activewear brand grew over a 130% in the region. Sheridan said that while China remains a small percentage of Brooks' business, the 'everyone's a runner' positioning and multi-use clothing consumers can wear off the run is driving growth.  Sheridan joined Bloomberg Businessweek from the floor of the Berkshire Hathaway shareholder conference in Om

stories.headline: What the Maine Senate Race Feels Like on the Ground
stories.summary: The anti-establishment mood is palpable in the state, home to one of the country’s top Senate contests. But Senator Susan Collins has some strengths.

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?
stories.summary: Colombia hosted nearly 60 countries at pivotal time on world stage for fight to transition to a clean energy futureLooking out to sea from the grey sandy beaches of Santa Marta, on Colombia’s Caribbean coast, it is never hard to spot evidence of the country’s thriving fossil fuel export trade. stories.headline: Antigua and Barbuda prime minister set to win fourth term in election
stories.summary: Gaston Browne is on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament after calling snap electionGaston Browne, the prime minister of Antigua and Barbuda, is set to win a fourth term in the country’s snap general election with preliminary results showing his party on course to win 15 of the 17 seats in parliament.Addressing supporters early on Friday morning, Browne said: “You have spoken, you have spoken clearly. You have indicated that the Antigua

stories.headline: UAE exit from OPEC signals closer alignment with US interests, experts say
stories.summary: UAE wants to supply more oil than its OPEC quota and that could help push down prices once the Strait of Hormuz opens.

stories.headline: Turkish police fire tear gas, arrest hundreds at May Day rally
stories.summary: Turkish authorities violently crackdown on May Day demonstrations at Istanbul’s Taksim Square, firing tear gas.

stories.headline: Takaichi's musical diplomacy with Indonesia shows fresh bid for Asia partners
KKR bullish on sports investment, Asian stadium deals: co-CEO
Scandal-hit 'baijiu' maker sharply revises earnings with chairman missing
Japan's Sumitomo Corp. to sell its Madagascar nickel mine business
Trump says US studying troop cuts in Germany, as spat with Merz intensifies
stories.summary: His remarks come after the German chancellor criticised the US approach to the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
stories.summary: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

stories.headline: Trump, Iran are locked in high-stakes standoff as oil prices hit 4-year high
stories.summary: As the U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. gas prices also reached a level not seen since just after Russia’s Ukraine invasion.

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
stories.summary: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup.The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a sym

stories.headline: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
stories.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
stories.summary: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

stories.headline: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
stories.summary: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.

stories.headline: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
stories.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.

stories.headline: Elon Musk and Sam Altman bring their rivalry to court
stories.summary: Of the two unloved billionaires, which will the jury trust?

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.
